summary: Salida's verified affordable-housing brief now rests on direct URL fetches for Salida Ridge / Confluent Park, voter-approved STR housing revenue, fee-in-lieu policy changes, South Ark / Vandaveer Ranch planning, Chaffee Housing Authority and Chaffee Housing Trust context, Jane's Place, and the Flour Mill / downtown-apartment pipeline.

sections.heading: Salida Ridge / Confluent Park LIHTC project

paragraphs.text: CHFA says Salida Ridge Apartments provides 48 affordable rental units for Salida residents with incomes between 30% and 50% of AMI, was developed by Commonwealth Development Corporation, and received a $1,220,000 federal 9% Housing Tax Credit reservation in 2020 plus $1,611,000 in permanent financing in 2021. Ark Valley Voice identified the project as Confluent Park Apartments, reported the 24 one-bedroom / 21 two-bedroom / three three-bedroom mix, and described it as the first LIHTC development in Salida in more than 20 years.
Later CHFA award announcements reviewed directly here do not list Salida among the project locations in 2024 Round Two or 2025 Round One Housing Tax Credit awards.

Preliminary 2022 election reporting showed Salida Ballot Issue 2A increasing the annual short-term-rental license to $1,000 for affordable housing, passing 55% to 45%, and Ballot Issue 2B increasing the short-term-rental lodging tax from $4.82 per day to $15 for affordable housing, passing 51% to 47%.

In May 2024, Salida City Council voted 4-1 to reduce fee-in-lieu charges from the prior flat $17.40 per square foot rate. The Mountain Mail reported that the new formula lowered fees for smaller projects and that fee-in-lieu collections fund the city affordable-housing fund.

Ark Valley Voice describes South Ark as the remaining portion of the original 190-acre Vandaveer Ranch parcel, purchased by the City of Salida in 2004 for water rights. It also reports that the Salida Natural Resource Center Development Corporation was formed in 2009 to handle financing for the Forest Service building and that the roughly 100-acre South Ark parcel has 50 to 60 developable acres after wetlands, springs, drainages, and ditches are considered.

In coverage of Ordinance 2023-16, Ark Valley Voice reported that the South Ark master plan would allow up to 400 housing units, with 300 units deed-restricted and 200 units in the affordable category, deed-restricted for at least five years.

sections.heading: Chaffee housing organizations and ballot measures

paragraphs.text: Chaffee Housing Authority describes itself as a multi-jurisdictional housing authority formed in 2020 by Chaffee County, the City of Salida, and the Town of Buena Vista.
Chaffee Housing Trust separately describes its work as a Community Land Trust model that provides affordable housing and rentals to qualified low-income residents.

Preliminary November 2024 coverage showed Chaffee Housing Authority Ballot Measure 6A, a 0.5% sales tax for CHA housing projects, failing while Ballot Measure 6B, a de-Brucing measure to retain revenue for affordable housing, passed. In November 2025, Chaffee County voters rejected Ballot Measure 1A, which would have raised the county lodging tax from 1.9% to 5.9%, with 58.19% voting no.

CHA's Jane's Place page describes a half-acre mixed-use development near downtown Salida with four buildings and 17 transitional housing units, plus nonprofit community space and a social-enterprise coffee shop. CHA reported approximately $5.7 million secured for construction, including a $1.287 million DOLA award; Ark Valley Voice later reported a December 2025 ribbon cutting, 13 affordable-housing units plus four Molly House units, and a $1.3 million OEDIT community-revitalization grant.

Ark Valley Voice reported that Salida City Council unanimously approved the Flour Mill Planned Development at 6907 CR 105 in January 2024. The two-acre parcel owned by Rob Gartzman is planned for 69 housing units in a mix of single-family homes, duplexes, townhouses, and apartment buildings; Gartzman said 50% of units would be deed-restricted and priced at 120% AMI, with some at 100% AMI.
KRDO reported on December 31, 2025, that the Chaffee Housing Authority would receive $1.1 million for 20 apartments near downtown Salida.
